text: In this paper, we propose a collaborative game to crowdsource natural language referring expressions for objects in images. Our two-player game format achieves the unique benefit of producing referring expressions and verifying the corresponding objects at the same time. We evaluate the collected expressions and demonstrate that the resulting CrowRef corpus of 130,525 expressions for 96,654 objects in 19,894 images of varying complex natural scenes is not only much larger but also more diverse than existing REG corpora. We present a detailed study of the CrowRef corpus, which sheds light on the object descriptions used by humans when referring to visual objects in images. Based on our findings, we formulate an optimization-based approach to referring expression generation. We conduct experiments on three datasets and show the merits of our approach.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
